steps.title: Find the GCF of both terms
steps.content:
The original expression is $16x^3y^2 + 14xy$. To factor, find the greatest common factor of the two terms.
  • Coefficients: $\gcd(16, 14) = 2$
  • Variable $x$: minimum power is $x^1$
  • Variable $y$: minimum power is $y^1$
The GCF is $2xy$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Divide each term by $2xy$:
$$\frac{16x^3y^2}{2xy} = 8x^2y$$
$$\frac{14xy}{2xy} = 7$$
Factoring out $2xy$ from the original expression:
$$16x^3y^2 + 14xy = 2xy(8x^2y + 7)$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
